name: Pacemaker Rhythm
identifyBy: Pacing spike immediately before the QRS (ventricular pacing) - sharp vertical deflection
Wide QRS ≥120ms following each spike - LBBB morphology if RV apex pacing
Atrial pacing spike before P wave (if dual-chamber)
Fixed rate or demand mode - spikes appear when rate drops below set rate
Paced QRS: discordant ST-T changes expected (LBBB pattern)

measurements.rhythm: Regular (paced) or sinus with pacemaker backup
measurements.prInterval: If DDD: normal PR with atrial then ventricular spikes

management: Pacemaker rhythm itself: no intervention needed
Pacemaker malfunction: failure to pace (no spike), failure to capture (spike without QRS), failure to sense (inappropriate firing)
Magnet application: converts to fixed rate - confirms capture in demand pacemakers
Cardiology referral for any pacemaker dysfunction
ICD: similar appearance but also delivers shocks - check patient's device card
pearls: Sgarbossa criteria apply to paced rhythms just as to LBBB - concordant ST elevation in paced rhythm = possible STEMI
Pacemaker malfunction types: failure to pace, failure to capture, failure to sense, oversensing
Twiddler's syndrome: patient manipulation of pacemaker rotates leads - loss of capture
Rate-responsive pacemakers: increase rate with activity - not pacemaker malfunction if rate rises with exertion
Pacemaker-mediated tachycardia (PMT): endless loop tachycardia in dual-chamber devices - magnet breaks the loop

qaNotes: Pacing spike immediately precedes a broad paced QRS with discordant repolarisation.
